title: guilandina bonduc var. major
intro: Guilandina bonduc var. major, also known as the horse egg tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caesalpinaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Guilandina bonduc var. major can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
